findings.evidence: Lines 279-359 use only `MulAction.orbitRel_apply`, `Subgroup.mem_bot`, `one_smul`, `Quotient` induction, and orbit membership; nearby generic orbit quotient utilities already live under `namespace TauCeti.MulAction` at `SubgroupFiberOrbitQuotientGroup.lean:47-98`.
findings.fix: Add a public generic `MulAction.orbitRelQuotientBotEquiv : MulAction.orbitRel.Quotient (⊥ : Subgroup G) X ≃ X` with the `mk`, `symm`, equality, and `⊥ ≤ H` map lemmas, then define the deck-fibre API by specializing it.
findings.issue: `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientBotEquiv` and its basic simp/map lemmas specialize a theorem that only needs `{G X} [Group G] [MulAction G X]`, not `Deck p` or topology.

summary: The bottom-orbit quotient API is introduced at the deck-fibre level even though the construction and simp lemmas are purely generic group-action facts. Put the generic result in `TauCeti.MulAction` first and make the deck declarations thin specializations.
